libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: No Docker service or servlet container is required for meaningful unit coverage. Prefer lightweight stubs or dynamic proxies for HttpServletRequest/HttpServletResponse when exercising servlet-adapter classes. Do not use Jakarta Servlet APIs; this old Jackrabbit version uses javax.servlet.

deterministic_setup.reason:
The artifact POM declares javax.servlet:servlet-api as provided, and classes such as AbstractWebdavServlet, DavServletRequest, DavServletResponse, WebdavRequestImpl, WebdavResponseImpl, InputContextImpl, and OutputContextImpl reference javax.servlet types. Tests run outside a servlet container need 
...[truncated]

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8851 [Automation] native-image run fails for org.apache.jackrabbit:jackrabbit-webdav:2.0-beta4; label=fails-native-image-run; body_chars=8015

risks: Using jakarta.servlet instead of javax.servlet will not satisfy the library's binary references.
Skipping servlet-api still permits some XML/header/method utility tests, but leaves important WebDAV server/request/response paths unreachable.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: jackrabbit-webdav has central server/request/response APIs compiled against the legacy javax.servlet API, but servlet-api is a provided dependency and is not pulled into the test classpath by the normal artifact dependency.
